senses.definition: a formal agreement between parties, particularly to help one another

examples.x: The two countries signed a defense pact to ensure mutual protection against external threats.
The environmental groups entered into a pact to collaborate on climate change initiatives.
The companies formed a strategic pact to share resources and expand their market reach.
The leaders shook hands, sealing their pact to work together for the betterment of the community.
The treaty served as a historic pact, fostering peace and cooperation between the formerly rival nations.

senses.definition: one of the sides in a legal agreement or dispute
to put an end to an argument with someone in order to make peace with them

examples.x: They had a huge fight but managed to patch up before the day ended.
He made an effort to patch up with his old friend after realizing the misunderstanding.
Their relationship was strained, but a heartfelt conversation helped them patch up.

senses.definition: an offering made to become friends with someone after upsetting them
used to show that one has accepted that someone else's argument or opinion is valid

examples.x: Point taken, I'll strive for clarity and brevity in my writing.
Point taken, I'll provide additional evidence to support my argument.
Point taken, I'll revise those sections to make them more clear.

senses.definition: displaying approval, support, or agreement

examples.x: Her feedback was positive, praising the team's efforts.
He has a positive attitude towards the project, believing it will succeed.
The positive response from the audience encouraged the performers.
They took a positive stance on the proposed changes.
His positive remarks made everyone feel more confident.

senses.definition: in a way that shows a good or optimistic attitude, expressing approval, joy, or support

examples.x: She responded positively to the news of her promotion, expressing joy and gratitude.
The team reacted positively to the encouraging feedback from the supervisor.
The student approached the challenging task positively, believing in their ability to overcome it.
The community responded positively to the volunteer initiative, showing support and enthusiasm.
The patient's health improved positively after the successful treatment.

senses.definition: to say things in order to settle a dispute or disagreement

examples.x: His ideas caused real dissension within the party at first, but he poured oil on troubled waters in last night's speech.

senses.definition: used to express complete agreement
an agreement between a couple before marriage in which they agree how much of each other's property each will receive upon divorce or death
the original form of an agreement, particularly a treaty between states, etc.; an addition to a treaty or agreement
a condition that needs accepting before making an agreement

examples.x: She agreed to sell the property with the proviso that she can stay in the house until the end of the month.
The merger will proceed, but there's a proviso that all current employees retain their positions for at least a year.

senses.definition: to forget a feeling, disagreement, or dispute

examples.x: After years of rivalry, the two competitors decided to put aside their differences and collaborate.
She knew it was important to put aside her resentment to maintain peace in the family.
Despite their past arguments, they managed to put aside their grievances and work together.

senses.definition: used to express emphasis or strong agreement
the act of validating an agreement by signing it or voting for it

examples.x: The treaty's ratification was completed after several months of negotiations.
The agreement became official following its ratification by the president.
The committee celebrated the ratification of the new bylaws.
The contract was not effective until its ratification by both parties.
Ratification of the amendment took place during the annual general meeting.

senses.definition: to formally approve a decision, action, etc., typically through an official process or legal means

examples.x: The members of the parliament convened to ratify the newly proposed constitution for the country.
After lengthy negotiations, the countries involved in the trade agreement finally ratified the terms.
The union members voted overwhelmingly to ratify the proposed collective bargaining agreement with the company.
Following the peace talks, the warring factions signed an accord to ratify the ceasefire and initiate the peace process.
The board of directors met to ratify the merger agreement between the two companies, officially sealing the deal.

senses.definition: to change one's opinions, beliefs, etc. to be like those of another person or group

examples.x: After the meeting, she decided to realign her business strategy with the new company goals.
The politician realigned his stance to better reflect the views of his constituents.
They realigned their marketing strategies to match the evolving preferences of their customers.
The team had to realign their project objectives to match the client's updated requirements.
To foster better collaboration, the departments realigned their priorities with the company’s vision.

senses.definition: the action of changing one's opinions, beliefs, etc. to be like those of another person or group
(of disagreements or differences) able to be settled
to make a person become friendly again with another after ending a disagreement or dispute

examples.x: The manager helped reconcile the team members after their conflict.
The peace treaty was signed to reconcile the two warring nations.
The mediator helped reconcile the two business partners after their falling out.
The diplomat’s efforts helped reconcile the conflicting parties.

senses.definition: the act of becoming friendly with someone once more after ending a disagreement
the act of resolving a problem or disagreement

examples.x: The conference aimed to find a resolution for environmental challenges in urban areas.
After hours of negotiation, they finally reached a resolution to the dispute.

senses.definition: (of a meeting, committee, etc.) to come to a decision through a formal vote

examples.x: After several hours of discussion, the council resolved to approve the proposed law.
The assembly resolved to reject the motion after voting unanimously against it.
They resolved to change the regulations following a formal vote during the meeting.
The team resolved that a postponement of the event was in everyone’s best interest.
The executive board resolved that the proposal did not meet the necessary requirements.

senses.definition: used to show one's agreement
used to show one's strong support or approval

examples.x: Right on, team!

senses.definition: to finalize a contract, deal, or agreement

examples.x: After weeks of negotiations, the two parties finally sealed the business partnership.
The real estate agent helped the buyers and sellers seal the deal on the purchase of the property.
The CEO traveled to the client's office to seal the multimillion-dollar contract.
The lawyers reviewed the terms of the settlement and advised their clients on how to seal the agreement.
The board of directors convened a meeting to seal the merger between the two companies.

senses.definition: to bring a dispute or disagreement to an end
an official agreement that puts an end to a dispute

examples.x: The two companies reached a settlement that resolved the patent infringement lawsuit.
After months of negotiations, the labor union and management finally signed a settlement to avoid a strike.
The divorce settlement included the division of assets and custody arrangements for their children.
The government brokered a settlement between the warring factions to bring peace to the region.
The settlement required the defendant to pay a substantial sum to the plaintiff to settle the legal dispute.

senses.definition: to shake hands as an act of agreement

examples.x: The two friends shook on their agreement to split the costs evenly.
The business partners shook on the new contract to finalize their agreement.
They shook on the bet to seal their friendly challenge.

senses.definition: together working toward a shared aim

examples.x: The team members worked shoulder to shoulder, combining their skills and efforts to achieve victory in the championship.
In the face of adversity, the community came together, standing shoulder to shoulder, to rebuild after the natural disaster.
The activists marched shoulder to shoulder, demanding justice and equality for all.
During the difficult times, the friends stood shoulder to shoulder, supporting each other through thick and thin.
The volunteers worked shoulder to shoulder, tirelessly helping those in need and making a positive difference in their community.

senses.definition: to support a person or group against someone else in a fight or argument

examples.x: He sided with the critics, expressing doubts about the new policy.
The employee decided to side with the opposition during the negotiation.
The jury unanimously sided with the plaintiff in the courtroom.
The citizens rallied together, choosing to side with environmental conservation.
The public tended to side with the underprivileged in the social justice debate.

senses.definition: to write one's name or mark on a document to indicate acceptance, approval, or endorsement of its contents

examples.x: The author regularly signs copies of her books at book signings.
The author excitedly signed copies of the newly published book at the book signing event.
The artist signed the painting in the bottom right corner.
Right now, the executive is actively signing letters for the upcoming mailing.

senses.definition: a person, organization, or country that has signed a formal agreement

examples.x: The signatories of the treaty agreed to uphold the terms outlined in the document.
As a signatory to the climate accord, the country is obligated to reduce carbon emissions.
The signatory organizations were held accountable for the implementation of the agreed-upon policies.
The signatory of the document confirmed their support for the proposed changes.
Several countries acted as signatories to the peace agreement, ensuring their commitment to the terms.

senses.definition: to sign a contract agreeing to work for someone
to sign a contract agreeing to do a job

examples.x: The freelancer decided to sign up for a year-long contract with the client.
The team eagerly signed up for the upcoming championship.
She decided to sign up for the position after reading the job description.
The aspiring actors happily signed up for the audition, hoping for a breakthrough.
He was excited to sign up as the new project manager for the company.

senses.definition: to put aside disagreements
having a uniform color without any patterns, gradients, or mixed shades

examples.x: She wore a solid blue dress to the party.
The walls were painted a solid white to make the room look bigger.
He prefers solid colors for his car, without any flashy designs.
The artist used a solid red background for the painting to highlight the figures.
The brand’s logo is usually printed in solid black for a sleek look.

senses.definition: the support given by the members of a group to each other because of sharing the same opinions, feelings, goals, etc.

examples.x: The workers stood together in solidarity to demand fair wages and better working conditions.
The community showed solidarity with the victims of the natural disaster by organizing relief efforts.
Students formed a union to demonstrate solidarity against proposed tuition hikes.
The rally was a show of solidarity with marginalized groups, advocating for equal rights.
The team members expressed solidarity with their captain, supporting her decision to retire.

senses.definition: used to show agreement with a suggestion

word_list.word: to stand up and be counted

senses.definition: to publicly express one's agreement or support for someone or something
to make an agreement that involves both parties doing something for one another
the state or act of accepting defeat and not having a choice but to obey the person in the position of power

examples.x: The rebels were forced into submission after their stronghold was captured by government forces.
His quiet submission to the harsh demands of his boss was noticed by his sympathetic colleagues.
The conquered nation was brought to submission, agreeing to all the terms imposed by the victors.
The athlete's submission in the final round of the match surprised many of his fans.
Her submission to the authority of the ruling party was evident in her compliance with their policies.

senses.definition: to support or agree with an idea, opinion, etc.
used as a positive response to something
showing care and understanding toward other people, especially when they are not feeling good

examples.x: The teacher was sympathetic to the student who was struggling with the coursework.
She was very sympathetic when I told her about my difficulties at work.
The doctor was sympathetic to the patient's concerns about the upcoming surgery.
Despite their differences, they were always sympathetic to each other's feelings.
The therapist provided a sympathetic environment for her clients to share their emotions.

senses.definition: in a manner that displays support or understanding

examples.x: The friends listened sympathetically to her struggles, offering comfort.
The manager responded sympathetically to the employee's personal challenges.
She nodded sympathetically as her friend shared a difficult experience.
The teacher spoke sympathetically to the students affected by the sudden change.
The community rallied sympathetically around the family in times of adversity.

senses.definition: to support and approve of something or someone
